source_article.title_en: Fantasy Baseball Week 14 Preview: Top 10 sleeper hitters include Paul Goldschmidt, Alec Bohm
source_article.author: Scott White
source_article.dek_en: Sleeper hitters for Fantasy Week 14, waiver wire targets, and breakout candidates.
source_article.body_en:
Whether you're in need of an injury fill-in or just a hot-hand play, Scott White has you covered with 10 sleeper hitters for the upcoming scoring period, all rostered in less than 80 percent of CBS Sports leagues. They're not must-starts by any estimation, but they're often the best you'll find off the waiver wire.

Be sure to check back Sunday for the latest updates.

1. Phillies @WAS4, @NYM3 2. Cardinals ARI4, MIA3 3. Orioles @LAA3, WAS3 4. Rays KC4, ARI3 5. Royals @TB4, @CHW3

1. Pirates SEA3, CIN3 2. Yankees @DET3, @BOS4 3. Tigers NYY3, HOU4 4. Guardians @CHW3, SEA3 5. Reds MIL3, @PIT3

1. Paul Goldschmidt (1B) - While Paul Goldschmidt might be the most under-rostered hitter in Fantasy right now, having reclaimed an everyday role while batting .382 with five home runs and a 1.087 OPS so far in June, it's hard to look past a schedule that ranks the second-worst for hitters this week. The reason I'm willing to do so is that there are five left-handers on it, and Goldschmidt has hit lefties to the tune of a .400 (26 for 65) batting average and 1.232 OPS this year.
2. Alec Bohm (3B) - You wouldn't know it from his overall numbers, but Alec Bohm has been about as effective as he ever has over his past 36 games, batting .301 (40 for 133) with eight home runs and an OPS over .850. He's primed to take advantage of the most hitter-friendly schedule this week, which includes four games against the Nationals pitching staff.
3. Lars Nootbaar (LF) - Lars Nootbaar has throttled the ball to the tune of a 95.3 mph average exit velocity since coming back from dual heel surgery while continuing to walk at his usual high rate. He should be able to convert that into success with the second-best hitter schedule this week, particularly since only one of the opposing pitchers throws left-handed.
4. Mauricio Dubon (SS) - Mauricio Dubon has continued to play a Swiss army knife role for the Braves and has been particularly productive in June, batting .304 (14 for 46) with four home runs in his past 12 games. The Braves just missed being among the five best hitter matchups this week, scheduled to face pitchers such as Griffin Canning, Randy Vasquez, Trevor McDonald, Adrian Houser and a struggling Michael King.
5. Blaze Jordan (3B) - Blaze Jordan has so far had an easy time transitioning to the starting third base role for the Cardinals, combining high exit velocities with a low strikeout rate, as he did in the minors. The Cardinals matchups this week make it the perfect time to give him a try, featuring such mashables as Merrill Kelly, Zac Gallen, Ryan Gusto, and Tyler Phillips.
6. Willi Castro (2B) - Eligible at every infield position plus the outfield, Willi Castro makes for a handy hot-hand play right now, batting .354 (29 for 82) with three home runs and two stolen bases in his past 22 games. Only his first three games are at his hitter-friendly home, but his road series is against a bad Twins pitching staff.
7. TJ Rumfield (1B) - I've been reluctant to recommend Rockies hitters this year because they have yet to have a full week of home games, but their road matchups this week against Mike Paredes, Zebby Matthews and a struggling Taj Bradley are just as inviting. TJ Rumfield, like Willi Castro, has been swinging a hot bat lately, batting .344 (11 for 32) with three homers in his past eight games.
8. Marcus Semien (2B) - Marcus Semien has never generated high exit velocities, and in recent years, it's taken him until midseason, when the ball generally carries better, to come around. That seems to be happening again, seeing as he's batting .262 (17 for 65) with five home runs and an OPS over .850 in his past 18 games.
9. Bryson Stott (2B) - There needs to be another hitter who can take advantage of the Phillies' No. 1 hitter slate this week, and since Brandon Marsh is too rostered already, my choice is Bryson Stott, who's been hitting the ball harder than ever this year and is batting .342 (13 for 38) with four steals in his past 11 games. Now that he's playing virtually every day, the number of lefties on the schedule doesn't matter as much, but there happens to be only one.
10. Dominic Smith (DH) - Fantasy Baseballers didn't get to enjoy much of Dominic Smith's blistering start to the season because his playing time was so sporadic, and now that injuries have forced him into the lineup against lefties as well as righties, his numbers have come down. He's still batting .299 (41 for 137) against righties, though, and that's all the Braves are scheduled to face this week, with most being bottom-feeders in the Padres and Giants rotations.

player_cards.blurb_en: While Paul Goldschmidt might be the most under-rostered hitter in Fantasy right now, having reclaimed an everyday role while batting .382 with five home runs and a 1.087 OPS so far in June, it's hard to look past a schedule that ranks the second-worst for hitters this week. The reason I'm willing to do so is that there are five left-handers on it, and Goldschmidt has hit lefties to the tune of a .400 (26 for 65) batting average and 1.232 OPS this year.

You wouldn't know it from his overall numbers, but Alec Bohm has been about as effective as he ever has over his past 36 games, batting .301 (40 for 133) with eight home runs and an OPS over .850. He's primed to take advantage of the most hitter-friendly schedule this week, which includes four games against the Nationals pitching staff.

Lars Nootbaar has throttled the ball to the tune of a 95.3 mph average exit velocity since coming back from dual heel surgery while continuing to walk at his usual high rate. He should be able to convert that into success with the second-best hitter schedule this week, particularly since only one of the opposing pitchers throws left-handed.

Mauricio Dubon has continued to play a Swiss army knife role for the Braves and has been particularly productive in June, batting .304 (14 for 46) with four home runs in his past 12 games. The Braves just missed being among the five best hitter matchups this week, scheduled to face pitchers such as Griffin Canning, Randy Vasquez, Trevor McDonald, Adrian Houser and a struggling Michael King.

Blaze Jordan has so far had an easy time transitioning to the starting third base role for the Cardinals, combining high exit velocities with a low strikeout rate, as he did in the minors. The Cardinals matchups this week make it the perfect time to give him a try, featuring such mashables as Merrill Kelly, Zac Gallen, Ryan Gusto, and Tyler Phillips.

Eligible at every infield position plus the outfield, Willi Castro makes for a handy hot-hand play right now, batting .354 (29 for 82) with three home runs and two stolen bases in his past 22 games. Only his first three games are at his hitter-friendly home, but his road series is against a bad Twins pitching staff.

I've been reluctant to recommend Rockies hitters this year because they have yet to have a full week of home games, but their road matchups this week against Mike Paredes, Zebby Matthews and a struggling Taj Bradley are just as inviting. TJ Rumfield, like Willi Castro, has been swinging a hot bat lately, batting .344 (11 for 32) with three homers in his past eight games.

Marcus Semien has never generated high exit velocities, and in recent years, it's taken him until midseason, when the ball generally carries better, to come around. That seems to be happening again, seeing as he's batting .262 (17 for 65) with five home runs and an OPS over .850 in his past 18 games.

There needs to be another hitter who can take advantage of the Phillies' No. 1 hitter slate this week, and since Brandon Marsh is too rostered already, my choice is Bryson Stott, who's been hitting the ball harder than ever this year and is batting .342 (13 for 38) with four steals in his past 11 games. Now that he's playing virtually every day, the number of lefties on the schedule doesn't matter as much, but there happens to be only one.

Fantasy Baseballers didn't get to enjoy much of Dominic Smith's blistering start to the season because his playing time was so sporadic, and now that injuries have forced him into the lineup against lefties as well as righties, his numbers have come down. He's still batting .299 (41 for 137) against righties, though, and that's all the Braves are scheduled to face this week, with most being bottom-feeders in the Padres and Giants rotations.

ai_article.article_summary_zh_tw: 本週CBS體育網推薦十大潛力打者，全為少於80%球隊擁有率，適合尋找熱手或替補傷兵的選擇。

players.summary_en: Paul Goldschmidt is under-rostered but hitting .382 with 5 HR and 1.087 OPS in June and hits lefties very well, making him a sleeper despite a tough schedule.
players.summary_zh_tw: Paul Goldschmidt六月表現出色，對左投打擊率高，是本週潛力股。

players.summary_en: Alec Bohm has a .301 average with 8 HR in last 36 games and faces the most hitter-friendly schedule including four games vs Nationals this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Alec Bohm近期打擊穩定，面對本週最佳打者日程。

players.summary_en: Lars Nootbaar has high exit velocity returning from injury and benefits from a strong hitter schedule with only one lefty pitcher this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Lars Nootbaar復出後擊球速度快，本週打者日程佳。

players.summary_en: Mauricio Dubon has excelled in June batting .304 with 4 HR in last 12 games and plays a versatile role for the Braves with decent pitching matchups this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Mauricio Dubon六月表現亮眼，持續扮演球隊多面手。

players.summary_en: Blaze Jordan is adjusting well as Cardinals' starting third baseman with good exit velocity and low strikeouts, facing favorable pitching matchups this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Blaze Jordan適應良好，本週面對有利投手。

players.summary_en: Willi Castro is eligible at multiple positions, hitting .354 with 3 HR and 2 SB in past 22 games and benefits from a road series vs a weak Twins pitching staff.

players.summary_en: TJ Rumfield has hit .344 with 3 HR in his last 8 games and faces inviting road matchups this week against weaker pitchers.
players.summary_zh_tw: TJ Rumfield近期火熱，面對本週有利客場投手。

players.summary_en: Marcus Semien has improved midseason with a .262 average, 5 HR and 0.850+ OPS in last 18 games, consistent with his usual midseason surge.
players.summary_zh_tw: Marcus Semien如往年中季趨穩，近期打擊持續回溫。

players.summary_en: Bryson Stott is hitting .342 with 4 steals in last 11 games, playing nearly every day, and takes advantage of the Phillies top hitter schedule despite only one lefty pitcher.
